notes: Shankara, Vishvanatha, and Ramanuja all converge on the peer-default move: peers will not credit compassion as the reason for withdrawal; they will assign fear, because their own model of why warriors leave the field defaults to fear. The engineering layer preserves this as 'the peer-default for why does a senior leave a system mid-cutover is fear of finishing it.' The mahā-rathas as the specific competent-peer-class is preserved (not collapsed to general audience). The varṇa-specificity is honestly narrowed to the role-aligned-peer-network case. The engineering vocabulary (open-source maintainer, hiring panel, mentee) does not erase Sanskrit (mahā-rathas, bahu-mataḥ, lāghavam appear in the analog). One subtle point: the verse explicitly names competent peers (mahā-rathas) — peers who themselves were competent to judge. The engineering layer correctly restricts to 'technically competent peers' (open-source maintainers who reviewed your migration design) rather than generic LinkedIn connections. PASS on 7.4 and 6.8.
